titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/292

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=265 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:13.275236+00 | translation moa=264 status=live modified=2017-01-20 11:57:30.329213+00

div.p:
Hugo dei gratia Dunkeldensis Episcopus . Omnibus presens scriptum uisuris uel audituris eternam in domino Salutem .
Nouerit uniuersitas uestra nos gratis et diuine pietatis intuitu remisisse et inperpetuum quietum clamasse
Abbati et Canonicis de insula missarum Canum et Coneuetum . quod Clerici Ecclesie Dunkeld’ percipere con
sueuerunt apud Madernin de terra que Scotice dicitur Abtheyn . Ad huius autem quiete clamationis
Robur perpetuum Huic Scripto Sigillum notrum Apposuiumus . Teste Capitulo Dunkeld’.

Hugh by the grace of God Bishop of Dunkeld, to all who shall view or hear the present document, wishes eternal salvation in the Lord. You all should know that, out of grace and godly piety, we have remitted and quitclaimed in perpetuity to the abbot and canons of Inchaffray the cain and conveth that the clergy of the church of Dunkeld were accustomed to receive at Madderty from the land which in Gaelic is called ‘apdaine’. For the perpetual strength of this quitclaim, we have affixed our seal to this document. As witness, the Chapter of Dunkeld.
